8 SELECT users.user_email
FROM wp_posts AS transactions
INNER JOIN wp_postmeta postmeta_gateway
ON postmeta_gateway.post_id = transactions.id
AND postmeta_gateway.meta_key = 'ld_payment_processor'
AND postmeta_gateway.meta_value = 'paypal_ipn'
INNER JOIN wp_postmeta postmeta_price_type
ON postmeta_price_type.post_id = transactions.id
AND postmeta_price_type.meta_key = 'price_type'
AND postmeta_price_type.meta_value = 'subscribe'
INNER JOIN wp_postmeta postmeta_test_mode
ON postmeta_test_mode.post_id = transactions.id
AND postmeta_test_mode.meta_key = 'is_test_mode'
AND postmeta_test_mode.meta_value = '0'
INNER JOIN wp_users users
ON users.ID = transactions.post_author
WHERE transactions.post_type = 'sfwd-transactions'
AND transactions.post_status = 'publish'
GROUP BY users.user_email
ORDER BY users.user_email ASC
  1. StellarWP\L\S\D\DB::StellarWP\L\S\D\{closure}()
    wp-content/plugins/sfwd-lms/vendor-prefixed/stellarwp/db/src/DB/DB.php:144

21 SELECT SQL_CALC_FOUND_ROWS wp_comments.comment_ID
FROM wp_comments
WHERE ( ( comment_approved = '1' )
OR ( user_id = 1
AND comment_approved = '0' ) )
AND comment_post_ID = 11
AND comment_type NOT IN ('ld_review', 'note')
AND comment_parent = 0
AND comment_type != 'order_note'
AND comment_type != 'webhook_delivery'
AND comment_type != 'action_log'
ORDER BY wp_comments.comment_date_gmt ASC, wp_comments.comment_ID ASC

Configuration

The following PHP constants can be defined in your wp-config.php file in order to control the behavior of Query Monitor:

QM_DB_EXPENSIVE
If an individual database query takes longer than this time to execute, it's considered "slow" and triggers a warning.
Default value: 0.05
QM_DISABLED
Disable Query Monitor entirely.
Default value: false
QM_DISABLE_ERROR_HANDLER
Disable the handling of PHP errors.
Default value: false
QM_ENABLE_CAPS_PANEL
Enable the Capability Checks panel.
Default value: false
QM_HIDE_CORE_ACTIONS
Hide WordPress core on the Hooks & Actions panel.
Default value: false
QM_HIDE_SELF
Hide Query Monitor itself from various panels. Set to false if you want to see how Query Monitor hooks into WordPress.
Default value: true
QM_NO_JQUERY
Don't specify jQuery as a dependency of Query Monitor. If jQuery isn't enqueued then Query Monitor will still operate, but with some reduced functionality.
Default value: false
QM_SHOW_ALL_HOOKS
In the Hooks & Actions panel, show every hook that has an action or filter attached (instead of every action hook that fired during the request).
Default value: false
QM_DB_SYMLINK
Allow the wp-content/db.php file symlink to be put into place during activation. Set to false to prevent the symlink creation.
Default value: true
